properties.name: Canal Park
properties.description: Canal Park is a tourist and recreation-oriented district of Duluth, situated across from Downtown and connected by the famous Aerial Lift Bridge to Park Point. Packed with shops, restaurants, and lakefront views.

properties.name: Aerial Lift Bridge
properties.description: The Aerial Lift Bridge is Duluth's most iconic landmark. Built in 1905 as the first transporter bridge in the US, it was converted to a vertical lift bridge in 1929–30 and continues to operate today, rising to let ships pass.

properties.name: Spirit Mountain Recreation Area
properties.description: A full-season recreational mountain featuring wintertime downhill skiing and snowboarding, plus a summertime adventure park with a mountain coaster, zip lines, and more.

properties.name: Glensheen
properties.description: Glensheen, the Historic Congdon Estate, is a stunning 20,000 sq ft Jacobean-style mansion operated by UMD as a historic house museum. Tour the 39-room estate and beautifully landscaped grounds along Lake Superior.

properties.name: Great Lakes Aquarium
properties.description: Opened in 2000 on the Duluth waterfront, the Great Lakes Aquarium is a nonprofit dedicated to inspiring people to explore their connection to Lake Superior. Features live exhibits, otters, and hands-on experiences.

properties.name: Jay Cooke State Park
properties.description: About 10 miles southwest of Duluth, Jay Cooke State Park protects the lower reaches of the St. Louis River. One of Minnesota's ten most visited parks, it features a dramatic 13-mile rocky gorge, suspension bridge, and scenic hiking trails.

properties.name: North Shore Scenic Railroad
properties.description: Step aboard for seasonal narrated train tours that follow a historic transport corridor along Lake Superior's North Shore. Trips range from short scenic rides to longer themed excursions through stunning lakeside scenery.

properties.name: Enger Tower
properties.description: A five-story, 80-foot stone observation tower built in 1939 atop Enger Hill, 531 feet above Lake Superior. A gift from Norwegian immigrant Bert Enger, it offers sweeping panoramic views of Duluth, Superior, and two states.

properties.name: Hawk Ridge Nature Reserve
properties.description: A 235-acre nature reserve and bird observatory perched along East Skyline Parkway. Each fall, thousands of migratory raptors funnel along the Lake Superior shoreline here, making it one of the premier hawk-watching sites in North America.

properties.name: Chester Park
properties.description: One of Duluth's oldest parks, established in 1891, winding through a forested ravine along Chester Creek. Trails pass small waterfalls and towering white pines, opening to Chester Bowl — a wooded valley with a ski hill and picnic areas.
